Title: Weisbach, Werner
Overview: Professor of art history, Basel [1933]; Heinrich Wölfflin friend; Vienna School; contributed to an early-twentieth century re-evaluation of the Baroque style. Weisbach was a student of Robert Vischer. In 1924 Weisbach wrote the volume for the prestigious Propyläen Kunstgeschichte on baroque art, which he dedicated to theologian Ernst Troeltsch (1865-1923). Weisbach was one of the first to write an analysis of Italian Mannerism. While he did not appreciate it as an art movement (as Walter F. Friedländer, would beginning in his 1914 inaugural lecture at Freiburg), he made the connection with the abstracting and stylizing techniques of his own era, German Expressionism. His students included Richard Krautheimer, who wrote his dissertation under him.
